text.title: mq-idempotent-consumers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mq-idempotent-consumers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting the deduplication marker after the side-effect instead of before.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two workers receiving the same message simultaneously both pass the "not seen" check and both execute the side-effect. nx=True before execution is the only way to prevent concurrent duplicate execution.

failure-mode.description: Using a TTL shorter than the maximum message retention period of the broker plus the DLQ replay window.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 1-hour dedup TTL on a queue with 4-day message retention means replayed DLQ messages are no longer deduplicated after 1 hour. Set TTL to at least the broker retention period plus the expected replay lag.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to auto_ack=True because SDK examples use it. This silently loses messages on consumer crash.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force auto_ack=False in every consumer. The cost of an extra ack call is negligible; the cost of silent message loss is a production incident.

failure-mode.description: Agents publish inside the DB transaction before commit, producing phantom events when the transaction rolls back.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use the transactional outbox pattern. Never publish directly inside a DB transaction.

failure-mode.description: Agents claim Kafka EOS (exactly-once semantics) gives exactly-once without enabling the required configs: enable.idempotence=true, transactional producer, and read_committed isolation on the consumer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a config audit before accepting exactly-once claims. Kafka EOS requires coordinated config on both producer and consumer; a single misconfigured setting silently reverts to at-least-once.

failure-mode.description: Schemas drift: agents add fields to message payloads without bumping the schema version, causing old consumers to crash on deserialization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Block field additions without a migration note and a version bump in the schema registry. Require backward-compatible schema changes: add optional fields only, never remove or rename existing fields without a version fence.
